title: AlcHep — Analysis
text:
master comes from the shared pipeline. Throughout this analysis:

Exposure: etoh_hepatitis — alcoholic hepatitis (AH) vs. non-AH
Primary outcome (event of interest, cause = 1): death within 90 days
Competing risk (cause = 2): liver transplant within 90 days
Censoring (cause = 0): alive and not transplanted at 90 days / lost to follow-up

Time and status come from the shared pipeline variables time_to_death_90days_cmp / time_to_death_status_90days_cmp (0 = censored, 1 = death, 2 = transplant).


Show cohort filter
# Cause coding for the competing-risk event factor `event_cmp`:
#   level 1 "Censored"   -> censoring        (numeric code 0)
#   level 2 "Death"      -> OUTCOME OF INTEREST (numeric code 1)
#   level 3 "Transplant" -> COMPETING RISK   (numeric code 2)
# This ordering matters: tidycmprsk::crr() treats the first level as the
# censoring code, and `failcode` selects which remaining level is the cause
# of interest (all other non-censoring levels become competing risks).
analysis <- master %>%
  filter(!is.na(etoh_hepatitis)) %>%
  mutate(
    etoh_hepatitis = factor(etoh_hepatitis, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Non-AH", "AH")),
    event_cmp      = factor(time_to_death_status_90days_cmp,
                            levels = c(0, 1, 2),
                            labels = c("Censored", "Death", "Transplant"))
  )

title: AlcHep — Analysis
text:
master comes from the shared pipeline. Throughout this analysis:

Exposure: etoh_hepatitis — alcoholic hepatitis (AH) vs. non-AH
Primary outcome (event of interest, cause = 1): death within 90 days
Competing risk (cause = 2): liver transplant within 90 days
Censoring (cause = 0): alive and not transplanted at 90 days / lost to follow-up

Time and status come from the shared pipeline variables time_to_death_90days_cmp / time_to_death_status_90days_cmp (0 = censored, 1 = death, 2 = transplant).


Show cohort filter
# Cause coding for the competing-risk event factor `event_cmp`:
#   level 1 "Censored"   -> censoring        (numeric code 0)
#   level 2 "Death"      -> OUTCOME OF INTEREST (numeric code 1)
#   level 3 "Transplant" -> COMPETING RISK   (numeric code 2)
# This ordering matters: tidycmprsk::crr() treats the first level as the
# censoring code, and `failcode` selects which remaining level is the cause
# of interest (all other non-censoring levels become competing risks).
analysis <- master %>%
  filter(!is.na(etoh_hepatitis)) %>%
  mutate(
    etoh_hepatitis = factor(etoh_hepatitis, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Non-AH", "AH")),
    event_cmp      = factor(time_to_death_status_90days_cmp,
                            levels = c(0, 1, 2),
                            labels = c("Censored", "Death", "Transplant"))
  )

title: AlcHep — Analysis
text:
Unadjusted Fine-Gray — 90-day mortality (AH vs. non-AH)
Outcome: death within 90 days (cause 1)  ·  Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)


Show unadjusted Fine-Gray
# Outcome of interest = "Death"; competing risk = "Transplant"
# (`failcode` is matched against the levels of `event_cmp`).
fg_unadj <- tidycmprsk::crr(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, event_cmp) ~ etoh_hepatitis,
  data     = analysis,
  failcode = "Death"   # cause of interest -> all other non-censoring levels are competing risks
)

fg_unadj %>%
  tbl_regression(exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  modify_caption(
    "Unadjusted subdistribution hazard ratio — Outcome: death (cause 1); Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)"
  )






Unadjusted subdistribution hazard ratio <U+2014> Outcome: death (cause 1); Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)








Characteristic
HR1
95% CI1
p-value




etoh_hepatitis








    Non-AH
—
—




    AH
1.54
0.98, 2.43
0.061



1 HR = Hazard Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval








Show unadjusted Fine-Gray
# Aalen–Johansen non-parametric CIF (used for both the plot and the day-90 estimates below)
ci_unadj <- cuminc(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, event_cmp) ~ etoh_hepatitis,
  data = analysis
)

ci_unadj %>%
  ggcuminc(outcome = "Death") +
  labs(
    title    = "Unadjusted 90-day cumulative incidence of death",
    subtitle = "AH vs. non-AH",
    x        = "Days from admission",
    y        = "Cumulative incidence — Death",
    caption  = "Outcome: death (cause 1)  |  Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)"
  ) +
  add_confidence_interval() +
  add_risktable() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 90, by = 30),
                     limits = c(0, 90),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(face = "italic"))










90-day cumulative incidence of death (AH vs. non-AH)
Non-parametric Aalen–Johansen estimator at t = 90 days, accounting for liver transplant as a competing risk. 95% CIs use the log–log transformation.


Show 90-day CIF table
ci90_raw <- ci_unadj %>%
  tidy(times = 90) %>%
  filter(outcome == "Death")

ci90_raw %>%
  transmute(
    Group              = strata,
    `CIF at 90d (%)`   = sprintf("%.1f", 100 * estimate),
    `95% CI (%)`       = sprintf("%.1f – %.1f", 100 * conf.low, 100 * conf.high)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    align   = "lrr",
    caption = "Aalen–Johansen 90-day cumulative incidence of death (competing risk: liver transplant)."
  )



Aalen<U+2013>Johansen 90-day cumulative incidence of death (competing risk: liver transplant).


Group
CIF at 90d (%)
95% CI (%)




Non-AH
39.6
32.4 <U+2013> 46.7


AH
50.1
36.2 <U+2013> 62.5






Show inline sentence
ah  <- ci90_raw %>% filter(strata == "AH")
nah <- ci90_raw %>% filter(strata == "Non-AH")

cat(glue::glue(
  "At 90 days the cumulative incidence of death accounting for liver transplant ",
  "as a competing risk was **{sprintf('%.1f', 100*ah$estimate)}%** in the AH group ",
  "(95% CI {sprintf('%.1f', 100*ah$conf.low)}–{sprintf('%.1f', 100*ah$conf.high)}) ",
  "and **{sprintf('%.1f', 100*nah$estimate)}%** in the non-AH group ",
  "(95% CI {sprintf('%.1f', 100*nah$conf.low)}–{sprintf('%.1f', 100*nah$conf.high)})."
))

At 90 days the cumulative incidence of death accounting for liver transplant as a competing risk was 50.1% in the AH group (95% CI 36.2<U+2013>62.5) and 39.6% in the non-AH group (95% CI 32.4<U+2013>46.7).

title: AlcHep — Analysis
text:
Adjusted Fine-Gray — MELD 3.0 model
Outcome: death within 90 days (cause 1)  ·  Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)  ·  Covariates: age, sex_male, hispanic, admit_meld_3, etoh_hepatitis.


Show MELD 3.0 adjusted Fine-Gray
# Outcome of interest = "Death"; competing risk = "Transplant"
tidycmprsk::crr(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, event_cmp) ~
    age + sex_male + hispanic + admit_meld_3 + etoh_hepatitis,
  data     = analysis,
  failcode = "Death"   # competing risk: "Transplant"
) %>%
  tbl_regression(exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  modify_caption(
    "Adjusted (MELD 3.0) subdistribution hazard ratio — Outcome: death (cause 1); Competing risk: liver transplant (cause 2)"
  )

Show adjusted CIF (MELD 3.0 model)
cif_meld3 <- adjusted_cif(
  prodlim::Hist(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp) ~
    age + sex_male + hispanic + admit_meld_3 + etoh_hepatitis,
  data = analysis
)

plot_adjusted_cif(
  cif_meld3,
  title    = "Adjusted 90-day CIF of death — MELD 3.0 model",
  subtitle = "Continuous covariates at median, categoricals at modal level"
)

title: Derived Variables
text: All variables computed in Tables.Rmd (and replicated in revision 0311.Rmd) from the REDCap source data.

title: Derived Variables
text:
Identifiers & Filtering








Variable
Description
Logic




id
Internal row ID per center
paste0("{center}_", row_number())


Discont_Day
Day terlipressin discontinued (0–13)
First day where discont_terli_dayN == 1 via qc_check()


center_id
Center abbreviation
str_extract(subjectid, "^[^-]+")


center
Numeric center grouping (factor, 1–10)
BID/MGH/Yale=1, PEN=2, OCH=3, BSW=4, CPM/CSF/UNM=5, Mayo/MCM/NWM=7, MMC=9, CCF/INU=10

title: Derived Variables
text:
Baseline Creatinine & eGFR








Variable
Description
Logic




baseline_scr_calculated
Estimated SCr from eGFR=75
Inverse CKD-EPI 2021 via numerical optimization (optimize())


baseline_scr_new
Best available baseline SCr
(1) baseline_scr if days_to_admit != 0, else (2) baseline_scr_120to365_value, else (3) min(calculated, scr_admit)


baseline_scr_new_2
Strict baseline (no calculated fallback)
Only options (1) and (2) above


eGFR
Estimated GFR
CKD-EPI 2021 from baseline_scr_new_2


ckd_baseline
Baseline CKD
eGFR < 60 → 1

title: Derived Variables
text:
AKI Staging








Variable
Description
Logic




ratio
SCr ratio at terli initiation
scr_terli_day0 / baseline_scr_new


delta
SCr delta at terli initiation
scr_terli_day0 - baseline_scr_new


akin
AKIN stage at initiation
1: ratio 1.5–2 OR delta > 0.3; 2: ratio 2–3; 3: ratio ≥ 3 OR SCr ≥ 4


discont_ratio
SCr ratio at discontinuation
lowest_creatinine / baseline_scr_new


discont_delta
SCr delta at discontinuation
lowest_creatinine - baseline_scr_new


discont_aki_stage
AKIN stage at discontinuation
Same staging as akin, using lowest_creatinine


difference
AKI stage change
akin - discont_aki_stage

title: Derived Variables
text:
Clinical Scores

Child-Pugh Score



Variable
Description




child_pugh_score
Sum of 5 components (5–15)









NoteComponent scoring








Component
1 point
2 points
3 points




Bilirubin (mg/dL)
< 2
2–3
> 3


Albumin (g/dL)
> 3.5
2.8–3.5
< 2.8


INR
< 1.7
1.7–2.3
> 2.3


Ascites
Absent (1)
Slight (2)
Moderate (3)


HE grade
None (0)
Grade 1–2
Grade 3–4








CLIF-C Score







Variable
Description




clif_c_score
10 * (0.33 * organ_score + 0.04 * age + 0.63 * log(WBC) - 2)


clif_c_score_new
Median-imputed (missing → population median)









NoteOrgan sub-scores














Organ
1 point
2 points
3 points




Liver (T.bili mg/dL)
< 6
6–12
≥ 12


Kidney (SCr mg/dL)
< 2
2–3.5
≥ 3.5


Brain (HE grade)
1
2–3
4–5


Coagulation (INR)
< 2
2–2.5
≥ 2.5


Circulation
MAP ≥ 70, no pressors
MAP < 70, no pressors
Any pressors


Respiratory (SpO2/FiO2)
> 357
214–357
≤ 214








ACLF Grade & Organ Failures








Variable
Description
Logic




aclf_grade
Organ failure count (capped at 3)
Count of organ failures below


organ_failure_sum
Total organ failure count (uncapped)
Sum of 6 binary flags


liver_f
Liver failure
bili_terli_day0 >= 6


brain_f
Brain failure
he_terli_day0 > 1


coagulation_f
Coagulation failure
inr_terli_day0 >= 2


circulatory_f
Circulatory failure
map_terli_day0_time0 < 70


respiratory_f
Respiratory failure
spo2_terli_day0 / fio2_terli_day0 <= 357


kidney_f
Kidney failure
scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5





MELD Scores








Variable
Description
Formula




day0_meld
MELD at Day 0
9.57*log(Cr) + 3.78*log(bili) + 11.20*log(INR) + 6.43; Cr capped 1–4


day0_meld_na
MELD-Na at Day 0
MELD + sodium correction (Na capped 125–137); applied only if MELD > 11


day0_meld_3
MELD 3.0 at Day 0
Includes sex factor (+1.33 if female), albumin, sodium, and bilirubin-sodium / albumin-Cr interactions


admit_meld
MELD at admission
Same as day0_meld using admission labs


admit_meld_na
MELD-Na at admission



admit_meld_3
MELD 3.0 at admission
Median-imputed if missing


admit_meld_3_raw
MELD 3.0 raw (pre-imputation)

title: Derived Variables
text:
Outcome Variables

HRS Response








Variable
Description
Logic




lowest_creatinine
Lowest SCr around discontinuation
min(last_scr_2, last_scr, scr_day1_post_terli)


last_scr
SCr on discontinuation day
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day}


last_scr_2
SCr day before discontinuation
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day - 1}


HRS_reversal_new
Primary outcome
lowest_creatinine <= 1.5 AND (scr_day0 - lowest_creatinine) >= 0.3


hrs_responders
Response category
0 = No stage improvement, 1 = Partial (improved but SCr > 0.3 above baseline), 2 = Complete (≤ 0.3 above baseline)


hrs_responders_cat_2
Binary responder
0 = Non-responder, 1 = Any response


hrs_rebound
SCr rebound post-terli
scr_day1_post_terli - last_scr >= 0.3





Survival / Competing Risk

Intermediate Variables








Variable
Description
Logic




dc_death_status
In-hospital death
1 if disposition == 5, else 0


after_discharge_90days_death_status
Died within 90 days post-discharge
1 if dc_death_90days == 1, else 0


time_to_death
Days from terlipressin initiation to death or censoring
In-hospital death: days_to_discharge; post-discharge death: days_to_discharge + dc_days_to_death; lost to follow-up: days_to_discharge + days_to_lost_to_followup; alive at 90 days: days_to_discharge + 90 (intentional censoring). All minus days_to_terli_initiation_day0


time_to_death_status
Overall death indicator
1 = died (in-hospital or post-discharge within 90 days), 0 = alive or lost to follow-up





90-Day Death Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day death analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death, 90) – all follow-up capped at 90 days


time_to_death_status_90days
90-day death event indicator
1 = died within 90 days; 0 = alive at 90 days, OR died after 90 days (censored at 90), OR lost to follow-up
1 only if time_to_death_status == 1 AND time_to_death <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Transplant Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_transplant_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day transplant analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_transplant_cmp, 90)


time_to_transplant_status_90days
90-day transplant event indicator
2 = transplanted within 90 days; 0 = not transplanted within 90 days, OR transplanted after 90 days (censored at 90)
2 only if transplant_lt_status == 2 AND time_to_transplant_cmp <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Competing Risk (Death vs. Transplant)









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days_cmp
Competing risk follow-up time (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death_90days, time_to_transplant_90days) – whichever event came first


time_to_death_status_90days_cmp
Competing risk event indicator
0 = censored (alive, no transplant within 90 days); 1 = death within 90 days (death came first or tied); 2 = transplant within 90 days (transplant came first)
If death time ≤ transplant time: use death status; if transplant time < death time: use transplant status









NoteHow the competing risk status works






Patient dies day 30, no transplant: time = 30, status = 1 (death)
Patient transplanted day 20, alive at 90 days: time = 20, status = 2 (transplant)
Patient alive at 90 days, no transplant: time = 90, status = 0 (censored)
Patient dies day 50, transplanted day 40: time = 40, status = 2 (transplant came first)
Patient died after 90 days: time = 90, status = 0 (censored – death beyond window)
Patient lost to follow-up day 60: time = 60, status = 0 (censored)







Transplant








Variable
Description
Logic




transplant_lt
LT timing category
1 = During hospitalization (LT or SLKT), 2 = Within 90 days post-discharge, 3 = After 90 days (within 1 year), 0 = No transplant


transplant_slkt
Simultaneous liver-kidney transplant indicator
1 if slkt_during_hospital == 1 OR dc_lt_90days == 2 OR lt_type_1yr == 2, else 0


transplant_lt_status
Transplant status for competing risk
0 = no transplant, 2 = transplanted (coded as 2 for competing risk with death = 1)


time_to_transplant
Days from terlipressin initiation to transplant
NA if no transplant


time_to_transplant_cmp
Transplant time for competing risk (999 if no transplant)
999 serves as a large censoring time so pmin with death time selects death


transplant_outcome
Transplanted within 90 days (binary)
1 if time_to_transplant_status_90days == 2, else 0


listed_transplant
Listed for liver transplant
1 if lt_eval == 2 OR lteval_listingstatus == 1, else 0

Treatment Course Variables








Variable

Logic




discont_reason_terli
Reason at discontinuation day
discont_reason_terli_day{Discont_Day}


terli_ae_abdpain
Abdominal pain AE
From discontinuation day


terli_ae_nausea
Nausea AE
From discontinuation day


terli_ae_diarrhea
Diarrhea AE
From discontinuation day


terli_ae_volover
Volume overload AE
From discontinuation day


terli_ae_brady
Bradycardia AE
From discontinuation day


terli_7
Respiratory failure reason
discont_reason_terli == 7


terli_8
Ischemic event reason
discont_reason_terli == 8


terli_ae_respfail
Respiratory failure composite
terli_7 == 1 OR terli_ae_volover == 1


any_ae
Any adverse event
Any AE flag == 1


mean_map_day0
Mean MAP on Day 0
Average of 4 daily MAP time-point readings


dosechange_day
First day of dose adjustment
First day (2–13) with dosechange_terli_dayN == 1


dosechange_status
Any dose change
Binary: any change days 2–12


time_to_dosechange
Day of first dose change



total_albumin
Total albumin across treatment
rowSums(albumintotal_terli_day0:day13)

title: Derived Variables
text:
Criteria / Exclusion Flags








Variable
Description
Logic




criteria_1
Discontinued day 1 with no dose
total_terli_day1 == 0 AND discont_terli_day1 == 1


criteria_2
ACLF grade ≥ 3
aclf_grade >= 3


criteria_3
SCr ≥ 5
scr_terli_day0 >= 5


criteria_4
Respiratory compromise
SpO2/FiO2 < 357


criteria_5
High MELD 3.0
admit_meld_3 > 35


HRS_Criteria
Any criteria 1–4 met



HRS_criteria_3
Criteria 2, 3, or 4 (without 1)



HRS_criteria_meld
Criteria 2–5

Dose-Change Subgroups (revision 0311)








Variable

Logic




discont_before_day3
Discontinued before day 3
Discont_Day < 3


pc_change_before_day3
Dose changed days 1–3, continued > day 3



pc_nochange_after_day3
No change days 1–3, continued > day 3



pc_day3
Discontinued exactly day 3
Discont_Day == 3

title: Derived Variables
text:
Other








Variable
Description
Logic




fena
Fractional excretion of sodium
100 * (scr_admit * ua_na) / (sodium_admit * ua_cr)


urine_pc
Urine protein:creatinine ratio
uaprotein_hrs / ua_cr_value_hrs


race_2
Binary race
White or missing → 1, else 0


rrt_2
RRT binary (need-not-candidate = 0)
rrt == 2 recoded to 0


bili_cal
Bilirubin (coalesced)

title: Derived Variables
text:
Multiple Imputation (revision 0311)
Predictive mean matching (PMM), m=10 imputations, 50 iterations, seed=12345.
Imputed predictors: sex_male, scr_admit, tbili_admit, sodium_admit, inr_admit, albumin_admit, bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, map_terli_day0_time0, fio2_terli_day0, spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0, wbc_terli_day0
Post-imputation: Clinical scores (CLIF-C, ACLF grade, Child-Pugh, MELD) are recomputed on each imputed dataset.
Models fit: Fine-Gray competing risk models pooled with Rubin’s rules via run_pooled_fg(), stratified by HRS criteria, transplant listing status, and other subgroups.

title: REDCap Variable List
text: Complete variable list from the Terlipressin Database REDCap Code Book (PID: 43500). The database contains 18 instruments with 1500+ fields. Treatment day instruments (Day 0–13) share identical structure and are documented once below.

title: REDCap Variable List
text:
Demographics










#
Variable
Field Label
Type
Values / Unit




1
subjectid
Subject ID
text
e.g. BCM-001


4
admit_date
Admit date
date
Required


5
admit_admitservice
Admitting Service
categorical
1 = Medicine, 2 = ICU, 3 = Primary Liver Service, 4 = Surgical, 5 = Other


6
admit_admitroute
Location patient was admitted from
categorical
1 = Emergency Room, 2 = Transfer from outside hospital, 3 = Direct admission


7
date_birth
Date of birth
date
Required


8
age
Age
calculated
rounddown(datediff(date_birth, admit_date, "y"))


9
sex_male
Sex

title: REDCap Variable List
text:
Terlipressin Treatment Days 0–13
Each treatment day (Day 0 through Day 13) contains identical fields. Variables are suffixed _day0 through _day13. Below uses N as placeholder.

Terlipressin Treatment









Variable Pattern
Field Label
Type
Values




terli_date_dayN
Day N date
date



days_to_terli_initiation_dayN
Days from admission to day N of terlipressin
calculated



discont_terli_dayN
Was terlipressin discontinued?
binary
1 = Yes, 0 = No


discont_reason_terli_dayN
What was the reason of discontinuation?
categorical
1 = Adverse Effect (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea, volume overload, bradycardia), 2 = Achieved HRS Reversal (serum creatinine < or = 1.5 mg/dL), 3 = Worsening Kidney Function or Requiring Renal Replacement Therapy, 4 = Death, 5 = Transitioned to Hospice, 6 = Further Decompensation Requiring ICU care, 7 = Respiratory Failure, 8 = Ischemic Related Event (skin discoloration, cyanosis, organ ischemia), 9 = Other

Field Label
Type
Values

HRS response during hospitalization?
categorical
0 = No response, 1 = Partial (decrease in AKI stage but Scr >0.3 mg/dL above baseline), 2 = Complete (serum creatinine < 0.3 mg/dL of baseline)





Recurrent AKI










#
Variable
Field Label
Type
Values




1537
recurrentaki
Recurrent AKI during hospitalization after HRS recovery? (ADQI definition: meeting AKI criteria >0.3 mg/dL increase in serum creatinine within 48 hours or >1.5x increase within 7 days, after a minimum of 48 hours from HRS recovery)
binary
1 = Yes, 0 = No


1543
retreatment_terli
Retreated with terlipressin?
binary
1 = Yes, 0 = No





Nadir and Peak Serum Creatinine During Hospitalization










#
Variable
Field Label
Type
Unit




1547
nadirhospit_scr
Nadir serum creatinine during hospitalization
text
mg/dL


1550
peakhospit_scr
Peak serum creatinine during hospitalization
text
mg/dL





Renal Replacement Therapy










#
Variable
Field Label
Type
Values




1553
rrt
Renal replacement therapy?
categorical
0 = No, 1 = Yes, 2 = Need for RRT, but not a candidate


1554
rrt_modality
What was the modality of renal replacement therapy?
categorical
1 = Intermittent, 2 = Continuous


1555
rrt_continuous
Was renal replacement therapy continuous?
categorical

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text: Sub-project of the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry. Compares alcoholic-hepatitis (AH) and non-AH patients treated with terlipressin for HRS-AKI, focusing on 90-day mortality as a competing risk against liver transplant.

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text: Sub-project of the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry. Compares alcoholic-hepatitis (AH) and non-AH patients treated with terlipressin for HRS-AKI, focusing on 90-day mortality as a competing risk against liver transplant.

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text:
Exposure

etoh_hepatitis — alcoholic hepatitis at admission (AH vs. non-AH)

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text:
Outcomes

Primary: 90-day cumulative incidence of death, with liver transplant as the competing event

time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp

Secondary / descriptive: HRS response (hrs_responders_cat_2, HRS_reversal_new), transplant listing / receipt, adverse events, disposition

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text:
Analyses

Table 1 — Demographics and clinical characteristics stratified by etoh_hepatitis
Unadjusted Fine-Gray — 90-day cumulative incidence of mortality (AH vs. non-AH)
Adjusted Fine-Gray (MELD 3.0) — covariates: age, sex_male, hispanic, admit_meld_3, etoh_hepatitis
Adjusted Fine-Gray (ACLF) — covariates: age, sex_male, hispanic, aclf_grade, etoh_hepatitis

title: AlcHep — Alcoholic Hepatitis in HRS-AKI Treated with Terlipressin
text:
